messages.content:
You are an expert Technical Product Manager.
Your job is to analyze the user's issue (Title and Body) along with the provided source code context, and generate a User Story, Acceptance Criteria, and Tasks.

OUTPUT FORMAT REQUIREMENTS:
You MUST output ONLY a valid JSON object. Do not wrap it in markdown. Do not include any explanations outside of the JSON.
The JSON must strictly follow this structure:
{
  "title": "Short title of the story",
  "story_type": "software_feature", 
  "jira_issue_type": "Story",
  "jira_labels": ["backend", "frontend", "bug", etc],
  "jira_linked_items": [],
  "user_story": "As a [role], I want to [action] so that [benefit].",
  "acceptance_criteria": [
    "Given [precondition] When [action] Then [result]"
  ],
  "story_points": null, // Hoặc một số nguyên Fibonacci (1, 2, 3, 5, 8) đánh giá độ khó
  "tasks": {
    "be": ["Backend task 1", "Backend task 2"],
    "fe": ["Frontend task 1"],
    "qa": ["Test case 1"]
  },
  "definition_of_done": ["Code reviewed", "Tests passed"],
  "planning_status": "READY_FOR_SPRINT",
  "clarification_questions": [],
  "assumptions": [],
  "story_splits": [],
  "sprint_allocation": [],
  "context_sources": []
}

If the issue lacks so much context that it's impossible to deduce the required changes even with the provided code context, you may set "planning_status" to "NEEDS_CLARIFICATION" and provide your questions in "clarification_questions", leaving "tasks" empty. Otherwise, strive to generate concrete tasks.

ISSUE TITLE: AgentHost: Restore Checkpoint doesn't hide removed requests ISSUE BODY: - Click Restore Checkpoint to go back 1 request - The text is added to the chat input but the request is not hidden in the UI --- RAG CONTEXT (SOURCE CODE) --- --- File: vscode_ai_docs_full.md --- ## Spawn-Time Env Binding The agent host inherits its env vars at fork time. `IAgentHostOTelService` reads `process.env` once in its constructor and caches the resolved config. Changing a `chat.agentHost.otel.*` setting at runtime therefore has **no effect** on the currently-running agent host — the host must respawn (reload window / restart VS Code) to pick up the new value. This is the same model used by the rest of the agent host service surface. ===== FILE: D:\VSss-codedata\vscode\src\vs\sessions\browser\parts\mobile\contributions\AGENTS.md ===== --- File: vscode_ai_docs_full.md --- ### Agents window selector differences The Agents window does not use the regular workbench `.interactive-input-editor` wrapper. Selector checks that are scoped to that wrapper may return nothing even when the Agents chat input is focused. ```js // Regular-workbench-specific selector; do not assume this exists in Agents. document.querySelectorAll('.interactive-input-editor .view-line') // More useful checks in Agents. document.querySelectorAll('.view-line') document.activeElement?.className === 'native-edit-context' ``` The `Control+Meta+i` / `Control+Alt+i` focus shortcut still works; only the DOM shape after focus differs. --- File: vscode_ai_docs_full.md --- ### Hiding AI Features When Disabled When surfacing a UI AI feature, ensure the feature hides when `chat.disableAIFeatures` is set: - **UI Hiding**: Use `ChatContextKeys.enabled` in `when` conditions to conditionally show/hide UI elements (commands, views, menu items, etc.) - Example: `when: ChatContextKeys.enabled` in action/command registration - This context key is `false` when AI features are disabled - **Programmatic Hiding**: Check `IChatEntitlementService.sentiment.hidden` to determine if AI features should be hidden - `sentiment.hidden` is `true` when the user signals no intent in using Chat - This should not only disable Chat but also hide all of its UI This ensures consistency when implementing AI-powered UI functionality across the codebase. ===== FILE: D:\VSss-codedata\vscode\.github\prompts\build-champ.prompt.md ===== --- agent: agent tools: ['github/github-mcp-server/*', 'microsoft/azure-devops-mcp/*', 'todos'] --- --- File: vscode_ai_docs_full.md --- # Agent host unit tests For tests in this area that touch the SessionDatabase, they MUST use an in-memory database, not a real database file on disk. Use `SessionDatabase.open(':memory:')` and see the examples from existing tests. ===== FILE: D:\VSss-codedata\vscode\src\vs\platform\agentHost\test\auth-rework.md ===== --- File: vscode_ai_docs_full.md --- # Agent Host The agent host communicates via the Agent Host Protocol. The specification for this lives in a directory `../agent-host-protocol` as a sibling of the VS Code directory. If this directory doesn't exist, you should use the "ask questions" tool to ask the user if they want to clone `git@github.com:microsoft/agent-host-protocol.git` to that directory. After doing so, you should also prompt the user to add `file:////plugins/copilot-plugin` as a plugin in their `chat.pluginLocations` settings.

response:
```json
{
  "title": "AgentHost: Restore Checkpoint hides removed requests in UI",
  "story_type": "software_feature",
  "jira_issue_type": "Bug",
  "jira_labels": ["agent-host", "ui-bug", "chat"],
  "jira_linked_items": [],
  "user_story": "As a user, when I click Restore Checkpoint and navigate back to a previous request state, the requests that were removed/modified during that restore operation should be hidden from the UI so they don't clutter the conversation history.",
  "acceptance_criteria": [
    "Given a checkpoint has been restored with some requests removed or modified, When I view the chat history, Then all requests that were part of the restoration (removed/modified) are visually hidden",
    "Given I click Restore Checkpoint and navigate back one request, When the previous state is loaded, Then any requests that didn't exist in that checkpoint are not shown in the UI",
    "Given a restored checkpoint contains fewer requests than current state, When I scroll through chat history, Then only existing requests from that checkpoint are visible",
    "Given the restore functionality works correctly, When I verify the hidden requests count matches expected removed count, Then the number should equal total requests minus checkpoint request count"
  ],
  "story_points": 5,
  "tasks": {
    "be": [
      "Review AgentHost protocol specification for checkpoint/restore semantics",
      "Identify which requests were removed during restore operation in database/state",
      "Add backend logic to track restored vs current request state"
    ],
    "fe": [
      "Locate the chat history rendering component for Agents window",
      "Implement conditional rendering to hide requests that don't exist in restored checkpoint",
      "Update selectors per documentation (use '.view-line' instead of '.interactive-input-editor .view-line')",
      "Add visual indicator or tooltip explaining hidden/restored requests"
    ],
    "qa": [
      "Test restore checkpoint with various numbers of requests removed",
      "Verify hidden requests don't appear in chat history UI",
      "Confirm restored state matches expected checkpoint content",
      "Validate that hidden requests can be accessed via undo/redo if needed"
    ]
  },
  "definition_of_done": [
    "Code reviewed by peer developer",
    "All unit tests passing for AgentHost restore functionality",
    "E2E test case created and passing",
    "UI renders correctly with restored checkpoint state",
    "No console errors related to request rendering"
  ],
  "planning_status": "READY_FOR_SPRINT",
  "clarification_questions": [
    "What is the expected behavior when a restored request was completely removed vs. modified?",
    "Should hidden requests be accessible via undo/redo functionality?",
    "Is there an existing pattern for hiding UI elements in Agents chat that we should follow?",
    "Are there any edge cases with partial restores or multiple checkpoints?"
  ],
  "assumptions": [
    "The 'Restore Checkpoint' button exists and is functional",
    "Requests are stored in a way that allows identifying which were removed during restore",
    "The UI component for chat history can conditionally render based on request existence",
    "Hiding requests doesn't affect their accessibility or functionality"
  ],
  "story_splits": [
    {
      "name": "Backend: Checkpoint state tracking",
      "priority": "high",
      "description": "Add logic to track which requests existed in each checkpoint vs current state"
    },
    {
      "name": "Frontend: Conditional rendering",
      "priority": "medium", 
      "description": "Implement UI logic to hide requests that don't exist in restored checkpoint"
    }
  ],
  "sprint_allocation": [
    {"task": "Backend checkpoint tracking", "story_points": 3},
    {"task": "Frontend conditional rendering", "story_points": 2}
  ],
  "context_sources": [
    "vscode_ai_docs_full.md - Hiding AI Features documentation",
    "vscode_ai_docs_full.md - Agent host unit tests guidance",
    "D:\\VSss-codedata\\vscode\\.github\\prompts\\build-champ.prompt.md"
  ]
}
```
